source_pr.title: planner: use ordered index with is null predicate | tidb-test=pr/2368
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #54188 

Problem Summary: Properly classify columns with null predicates (e.g. `WHERE a IS NULL`) as constant so index selection can take advantage of that to satisfy a sort in https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/blob/432bb79f9732cb89a0be220ca93415df37a4849e/pkg/planner/core/find_best_task.go#L791.

### What changed and how does it work?
Added checks for is null predicate during planning and fill corresponding data structures marking that column as a constant.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: tk.MustQuery("explain select a from t1 where b is null order by c").Check(testkit.Rows(...)) (pkg/planner/core/casetest/index/index_test.go)
failure_signature: tk.MustQuery("explain select a from t1 where b is null order by c").Check(testkit.Rows(...)) (pkg/planner/core/casetest/index/index_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: Insufficient evidence to classify this as a flaky-test stabilization from the available case data. This PR primarily changes planner/ranger behavior to treat `IS NULL` predicates as constants so the optimizer can keep index order, and adds/updates test coverage; treat as a low-confidence/unclear flaky-fix signal.
fix_pattern: Planner/ranger correctness: treat `IS NULL` predicate as constant when extracting index access conditions so ordered index scans can keep order; add regression test `TestOrderedIndexWithIsNull`.

evidence: evidence: tk.MustQuery("explain select a from t1 where b is null order by c").Check(testkit.Rows( (pkg/planner/core/casetest/index/index_test.go)
